concerns: The mock initial thread and message seed data are directly declared inside the Svelte component (App.svelte). Separating them into a dedicated JSON or mock store file would improve component maintainability and readability.

The `fetchApi` function falls back silently to simulation on any fetch failure. While excellent for robustness and development mode, logging details or showing a subtle UI warning when actual backend requests fail would improve operator and developer troubleshooting.

The simulated WebSocket connection is initialized on mount and schedules a hardcoded timeout (15 seconds) to trigger simulated messages. In production and higher environments, this logic should be disabled or separated under a distinct development/simulation flag to avoid unexpected behavior.
